title: Call of Abraham
blurb: God calls Abram to leave his homeland for an unknown country, promising to make him into a great nation.

title: Covenant confirmed with Abraham
blurb: God renews his promise to Abram, telling him to count the stars, for so his descendants would be.

title: Joseph sold into slavery
blurb: Jealous of their father’s favorite, Joseph’s brothers sell him to traders bound for Egypt.

title: Joseph rises to rule Egypt
blurb: Joseph, once a prisoner, is elevated to govern Egypt and later welcomes his family into Goshen.

title: Birth of Moses
blurb: Moses is born under Pharaoh’s order to kill Hebrew infants, and hidden, then adopted from the river.

title: The Exodus from Egypt
blurb: After the plagues, Pharaoh finally lets Israel go, and the nation leaves Egyptian slavery for good.

title: The Law given at Sinai
blurb: At Mount Sinai, God gives Israel the Ten Commandments as the foundation of their covenant with him.

title: Fall of Jericho
blurb: Under Joshua, Israel enters the promised land and the walls of Jericho fall after seven days of marching.

title: Ruth and Boaz in Bethlehem
blurb: A Moabite widow named Ruth gleans in Boaz’s fields, and their marriage places her in the line of David.

title: Saul anointed first king
blurb: Samuel anoints Saul as Israel’s first king, in response to the people’s demand to be like other nations.

title: David anointed king
blurb: After years as a fugitive from Saul, David is anointed king over all Israel at Hebron.

title: Solomon’s temple completed
blurb: Solomon finishes building the temple in Jerusalem, and the ark of the covenant is brought into it.

title: Kingdom divides
blurb: After Solomon’s death, the nation splits into the northern kingdom of Israel and the southern kingdom of Judah.

title: Elijah at Mount Carmel
blurb: Elijah confronts the prophets of Baal in a public contest, and fire from heaven proves Yahweh is God.

title: Fall of Samaria
blurb: Assyria conquers the northern kingdom of Israel and deports its people, ending the northern monarchy.

title: Hezekiah and Assyria
blurb: King Hezekiah prays for deliverance as Assyria besieges Jerusalem, and the city is spared overnight.

title: Fall of Jerusalem
blurb: Babylon destroys Jerusalem and the temple, ending the kingdom of Judah and beginning the exile.

title: Exile in Babylon
blurb: Israel’s captives grieve their homeland by the rivers of Babylon, uncertain if they will ever return.

title: Return under Cyrus
blurb: The Persian king Cyrus decrees that the exiles may return home and rebuild the temple in Jerusalem.

title: Second temple completed
blurb: Decades after the return from exile, the rebuilt temple in Jerusalem is finally finished and dedicated.

title: Esther becomes queen
blurb: A Jewish orphan named Esther is chosen queen of Persia, setting up her later role in saving her people.

title: Nehemiah rebuilds the wall
blurb: Nehemiah leads the exiles who remained in rebuilding Jerusalem’s broken walls in just fifty-two days.

title: Birth of Jesus
blurb: Jesus is born in Bethlehem to Mary, fulfilling the ancient hope of a promised deliverer.

title: Jesus baptized, ministry begins
blurb: John baptizes Jesus in the Jordan, and a voice from heaven names him God’s beloved Son.

title: Crucifixion and resurrection
blurb: Jesus is crucified outside Jerusalem at Passover and rises from the dead on the third day.

title: Pentecost
blurb: Fifty days after the resurrection, the Holy Spirit fills the disciples and the church begins in Jerusalem.

title: Paul’s conversion
blurb: A blinding light on the road to Damascus turns Saul the persecutor into Paul the apostle.

title: Paul’s first missionary journey
blurb: The church at Antioch sends Paul and Barnabas out to preach across Cyprus and Asia Minor.

title: Council at Jerusalem
blurb: Apostles and elders meet in Jerusalem to settle whether Gentile believers must keep the Jewish law.

title: Paul imprisoned in Rome
blurb: Paul arrives in Rome as a prisoner awaiting trial, yet continues teaching about Jesus from his rented house.

title: Revelation on Patmos
blurb: Exiled to the island of Patmos, John receives the visions that become the book of Revelation.
